title: Add pseudocode display for all 11 distributed systems engines
description: I6 (Pseudocode Faithfulness) scores 0/3 for ALL 11 engines. This is the highest-impact implementation improvement. For each engine, write academic-style pseudocode that maps line-by-line to the implementation. Use standard textbook notation (CLRS/Raft paper style). Add a toggleable 'Pseudocode' panel in the UI that highlights the current line as the simulation advances.
acceptanceCriteria: All 11 engines have corresponding pseudocode
Pseudocode uses academic notation (not TypeScript syntax)
Pseudocode panel highlights current line during step-through
Each pseudocode has line numbers that can be cross-referenced
Pseudocode matches implementation logic

title: Fix Raft log visualization truncation — show all entries or indicate truncation
description: Visual lie: At DistributedModule.tsx:387, node.log.slice(-3) shows only the last 3 log entries. A student with 5+ entries sees an incomplete picture with no indication.
acceptanceCriteria: All log entries visible or truncation clearly indicated
Student knows when entries are hidden

title: Show virtual nodes in Consistent Hashing canvas visualization
description: Visual lie: ConsistentHashCanvas never renders virtual node positions despite the engine having 50 vnodes per physical node. The student cannot see WHY distribution is even. The orphaned ConsistentHashRingVisualizer.tsx:141-159 correctly renders vnodes as dotted circles.
acceptanceCriteria: Virtual node positions visible when toggled on
Toggle defaults to off to avoid clutter
Color of vnode dots matches their physical node

title: Add per-participant vote steps to 2PC visualization
description: Visual lie: At two-phase-commit.ts:95-101, all participant votes are computed in a single loop and displayed as one step. In real 2PC, the coordinator receives votes asynchronously. Generate per-participant vote steps.
acceptanceCriteria: Each participant's vote is a separate step
Coordinator 'waits' between votes (visual pause)
Step count increases from ~5 to ~7-8

title: Add key redistribution narration to Consistent Hashing
description: Consistent Hashing generates no step narration. When a node is added/removed, keys silently redistribute. The core concept — 'only 1/N keys move' — is invisible. Add narration: 'Adding Node 3... Key user:42 moved from Node 1 to Node 3. Total: 34 keys moved out of 100 (34%).'
acceptanceCriteria: Adding a node generates narration showing how many keys moved
At least 3 example key moves shown
Total redistribution percentage displayed

title: Add educational 'why' comments to Raft message handler implementations
description: The handleRequestVote (line 578), handleAppendEntries (line 654), and advanceCommitIndex (line 765) implement Raft's safety guarantees but lack 'why' comments. Add comments explaining the INVARIANT being maintained, referencing Raft paper section numbers.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 8 'why' comments in core Raft message handlers
Each comment references the Raft paper section
Comments explain the INVARIANT, not just the operation

title: Add legends to all canvas visualizations missing them
description: V4 (Legend Completeness) scores 0-1 for 6 of 11 visualizers. ConsistentHashCanvas (0), SagaCanvas (0), RaftCanvas (1), CAPCanvas (1), TwoPCCanvas (1), MapReduceCanvas (1), PaxosCanvas (1).
acceptanceCriteria: All 11 canvas visualizations have visible legends
Legends explain every color/state that appears
Consistent placement (bottom of canvas)

title: Add Raft step descriptions that explain state transitions
description: Event descriptions show post-mutation state without narrating the transition. 'starting election for term 1' should be 'Node-0 hasn't heard from a leader for 250ms. It increments its term from 0→1 and becomes a candidate.'
acceptanceCriteria: Election events explain WHY the timeout fired
Vote events explain the majority logic
Each event description includes BEFORE→AFTER transition

title: Restructure Paxos implementation for teaching clarity
description: The single simulatePaxos function (paxos.ts:58-227) uses a large if/else at line 75. Split into: (1) simulatePaxosSingleProposer() — happy path, (2) simulatePaxosCompetingProposers() — conflict scenario. Add comment explaining why competing proposers are the interesting case.
acceptanceCriteria: Two clearly named functions or modes
Each has header comment explaining the scenario
Comment at competing-proposer section explains 'why two phases exist'

title: Add Python display code for top 5 distributed systems engines
description: I7 (Multi-Language Readiness) scores 0/3 for all engines. Create Python equivalents for: (1) Raft election logic, (2) Consistent Hashing ring, (3) Vector Clock merge rule, (4) G-Counter + PNCounter, (5) Lamport timestamp rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Python display code for 5 engines viewable in UI
Python code matches TypeScript implementation logic
Syntax highlighted with line numbers
